title: Number of Equal Groups
essential_question: How can you model division to find how many equal groups?
strategies: Circle groups of the given size until all counters are used.
Think: total ÷ size of each group = number of groups.
Skip count up to the total.

pretest.question: 35 counters, 7 in each group. How many groups?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.3 'Number of Equal Groups' pp.229-232
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Division as Measurement (Quotative)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Finding the Number of Groups
learn.content: When you know the total and how many in each group, divide to find the number of groups. 24 ÷ 8 = 3 means 24 items, 8 in each group, makes 3 groups.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.3 'Number of Equal Groups' pp.229-232
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Division as Measurement (Quotative)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Model It with Circles
learn.content: For 35 ÷ 7: circle groups of 7 from 35 counters. First circle: 7. Second: 14. Third: 21. Fourth: 28. Fifth: 35. You make 5 circles. So 35 ÷ 7 = 5 groups.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.3 'Number of Equal Groups' pp.229-232
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Division as Measurement (Quotative)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Skip Count Up
learn.content: To find 36 ÷ 9: count by 9s → 9, 18, 27, 36. That's 4 counts. So 36 ÷ 9 = 4 groups. Skip counting shows you how many equal jumps fit into the total.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.3 'Number of Equal Groups' pp.229-232
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Division as Measurement (Quotative)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Number Line: Counting Groups
learn.content: Use a number line to find 24 ÷ 6. Start at 0, jump 6 each time: 0 → 6 → 12 → 18 → 24. Count the jumps: 4. So 24 ÷ 6 = 4 groups. Each jump is one group of 6.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.3 'Number of Equal Groups' pp.229-232
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Division as Measurement (Quotative)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: The Division Equation
learn.content: When you know the total and size of each group, write: Total ÷ Each = Groups. Example: 35 total ÷ 7 each = 5 groups. Compare to finding group size: Total ÷ Groups = Each. Both use division — only the unknown changes.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.3 'Number of Equal Groups' pp.229-232
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Division as Measurement (Quotative)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 측정(묶어 세기) 루틴 — 몇 묶음?
learn.content: 전체 ÷ 모둠당 크기 = 모둠 수. 예: 24개를 6개씩 묶으면? 24 ÷ 6 = 4묶음. 단계 1) 전체와 모둠당 크기 찾기 (전체 24, 한 묶음 6). 단계 2) 전체 ÷ 묶음 크기 식 (24 ÷ 6). 단계 3) 곱셈으로 풀기 (몇 × 6 = 24? → 4). 단계 4) 답의 단위 = '묶음' 또는 '모둠'.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.3 'Number of Equal Groups' pp.229-232
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Division as Measurement (Quotative)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 스킵 카운트로 풀기 — 30 ÷ 5의 빠른 방법
learn.content: 측정 나눗셈은 스킵 카운트와 자연스럽게 어울립니다. 예: 30 ÷ 5 → 5씩 세어 30 도달까지 몇 번?   5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30 → 6번 → 답 6묶음. 팁: 손가락으로 한 번씩 세면 정확. 흔한 실수 (M05): 시작 0을 빼먹거나 끝 30을 빠뜨려 한 개 차이로 틀림 — 끝 숫자(30) 셀 때 손가락 잊지 마세요.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.3 'Number of Equal Groups' pp.229-232
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Division as Measurement (Quotative)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 분배 vs 측정 — 식 세우기 비교
learn.content: 두 모델 모두 식은 같은 24 ÷ 6이지만 의미가 다름. 분배: '24개를 6명이 나눠 가짐' → 답 4 = 한 명당 개수. 측정: '24개를 6개씩 묶음' → 답 4 = 묶음 수. 🔍 핵심 구분: '나눠지는 사람/그릇 수'가 주어지면 분배, '한 묶음 크기'가 주어지면 측정. 양쪽 다 곱셈 검산은 같음: 4 × 6 = 24 ✓.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.3 'Number of Equal Groups' pp.229-232
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Division as Measurement (Quotative)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.explanation: 72 ÷ 8 = 9 rows. 3 rows go in = 3 × 8 = 24. 72 − 24 = 48 still outside.
hints: Step 2: 3 rows go in = 3 × 8 = 24 students. Step 3: 72 − 24 = 48 still outside.

feedback.correct: Great! 72÷8=9 rows. 3 rows × 8 = 24 go in. 72−24=48 still outside.

practice_r3.question: 72 students line up in rows of 8. The first 3 rows go inside. How many students are still outside?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Great! 72÷8=9 rows. 3 rows × 8 = 24 go in. 72−24=48 still outside.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.3 'Number of Equal Groups' pp.229-232
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Division as Measurement (Quotative)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Step 1: how many stickers did she give away? 40 − 10 = 30.

feedback.incorrect: She kept 10, so gave away 40−10=30. Then 30÷5=6 friends.

practice_r3.question: Emma has 40 stickers. She gives 5 stickers to each friend until she has 10 left. How many friends got stickers?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.3 'Number of Equal Groups' pp.229-232
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Division as Measurement (Quotative)
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.explanation: 30 ÷ 6 = 5 friends get all 30 cards. Jake has 0 left. Then 0 + 5 = 5 cards.
hints: Step 1: he gives ALL cards away (30 ÷ 6 = 5 friends get them all). He has 0 left.

feedback.correct: Correct! After giving all away Jake has 0. Then 0+5=5 cards.
feedback.incorrect: He gives ALL 30 cards away (to 5 friends), leaving him with 0. Then 0+5=5.

practice_r3.question: Jake has 30 cards. He gives 6 cards to each friend until all cards are gone. Then he buys 5 more cards. How many cards does Jake have now?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Correct! After giving all away Jake has 0. Then 0+5=5 cards.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.3 'Number of Equal Groups' pp.229-232
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic D — Division as Measurement (Quotative)
